title: Bad Bunny Makes Grammy History with Album of the Year Win
text: Bad Bunny achieved a historic milestone by becoming the first Spanish-language artist to win the Grammy Award for Album of the Year with his album Debí Tirar Más Fotos. The Puerto Rican artist defeated competitors including Justin Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter, Kendrick Lamar, and Tyler, the Creator in the prestigious category. An emotional Bad Bunny took the stage in tears. After composing himself, he expressed gratitude in Spanish to God, the Recording Academy, his mother, and everyone involved in creating the album. He then addressed the audience in English with a poignant message: I want to dedicate this award to all the people that had to leave their homeland, their country, to follow their dreams. At the same ceremony, Bad Bunny also won best música urbana album and best global music performance for EoO, plus earned a nomination for best album cover in a new category. This victory marks a significant breakthrough, as his previous album Un Verano Sin Ti made history three years earlier as the first Spanish-language release to earn a Grammy nomination for album of the year, though it lost to Harry Styles' Harry's House.
